name: farnesol
summary: Sesquiterpene alcohol from essential oils and mevalonate pathway with lipid-signaling modulating effects. Present in many aromatic plants; primarily studied in preclinical and cosmetic contexts.
description: Sesquiterpene alcohol from essential oils and mevalonate pathway with lipid-signaling modulating effects. Present in many aromatic plants; primarily studied in preclinical and cosmetic contexts.
safetyNotes: Essential-oil constituents can irritate skin/mucosa and may be toxic internally at high doses; use dilution and herb-specific safety.

claimMap.claim: Mechanism/background only; do not publish clinical benefit claims without human evidence.
claimMap.notes: Mechanism/background only; do not publish clinical benefit claims without human evidence.

effects: GABAergic or adrenergic signaling depending compound
primary_effects: GABAergic or adrenergic signaling depending compound
safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Pharmacological applications of farnesol (C15H26O): a patent review.

contraindications: Classified as a skin and eye irritant and a known fragrance sensitizer so topical or concentrated exposure can trigger allergic contact dermatitis in sensitive individuals
cytotoxic to cells at high in-vitro concentrations so formulation and dose matter for safety
human oral-supplementation safety data are limited so avoid high-dose isolated intake without clinician guidance.
